title: activity — PHP attribute contracts
description: Two attributes. `#[AsActivityFormatter]` on a class marks it as a description formatter for a specific log_name (registered via ActivityFormatterRegistry at boot). `#[AsActivitySubscriber]` on a service class registers it as a subscriber to `ActivityRecorded` events for a specific log_name (used by downstream modules that need to react to their own activities — e.g. digest queue routers).

attributes.name: AsActivityFormatter
attributes.description: Class-level marker on ActivityFormatter subclasses. Boot-time discovery scans #[AsActivityFormatter]-annotated classes and seeds them into ActivityFormatterRegistry with their log_name key. Only used when a domain module needs custom rendering beyond the default template interpolation (e.g. rendering a booking activity with a facility-name chip that resolves to a link). If no formatter is registered for a log_name, the default formatter interpolates properties in as `{{property}}` placeholders.

params.name: log_name
params.description: Log name this formatter handles. Must match a name in data/log-names-catalog.json. Refused at boot otherwise.

params.name: priority
params.description: Higher priority formatters win when multiple are registered for the same log_name. Rarely needed — one formatter per log_name is the norm.

params.name: supports_events
params.description: Which vendor events this formatter handles. `['*']` = all events. Otherwise a list like `['created', 'confirmed']` restricts the formatter to those events.

attributes.name: AsActivitySubscriber
attributes.description: Marks a service class as an ActivityRecorded subscriber. Boot-time discovery registers the class with Laravel's event dispatcher, filtered by log_name. Alternative to manual EventServiceProvider wiring — keeps activity-adjacent listener registration self-contained. Used sparingly — downstream modules typically consume via broadcast (WebSocket) or digest email; explicit subscribers are for programmatic reactions like 'when a booking is cancelled, kick off a refund workflow'.

params.name: log_name
params.description: Filter by log_name. NULL = every ActivityRecorded event fires the subscriber. Otherwise the subscriber only fires for activities matching this log_name.

params.name: events
params.description: Filter by vendor event key. `['*']` = all events.

params.name: queue
params.description: Queue name for the subscriber. NULL = sync (rarely correct — activity handlers should almost always queue).
